regions.text: There may be as many as 100,000 different sorts of proteins in a man’s body. A protein in our blood carries oxygen to the cells. Others make bones strong or muscles move.
Proteins in our hair, skin, and nails make them what they are. Others help in the digestion of food and keeping us healthy.
Proteins make children become surprisingly like their parents. nail: finger nail, toe nail. digestion: the changing of food so it can be used by the body.
